title: To Eva
content:
O fair and stately maid, whose eyes
    Were kindled in the upper skies
    At the same torch that lighted mine;
    For so I must interpret still
    Thy sweet dominion o'er my will,
    A sympathy divine.

    Ah! let me blameless gaze upon
    Features that seem at heart my own;
    Nor fear those watchful sentinels,
    Who charm the more their glance forbids,
    Chaste-glowing, underneath their lids,
    With fire that draws while it repels.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ralph Waldo Emerson, titled "To Eva", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
